stem: A HR analytics study surveys 30 staff members and observes 2 positive outcomes out of 30 (6.7%). Estimate the true employee turnover rate and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Sample proportion: 0.0667. Wilson 95% CI: [0.0185, 0.2133]. Point estimate: 0.1159.

common_failure_modes: Using the Wald interval (p̂ ± z√(p̂(1-p̂)/n)) which performs poorly for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
Not recognizing that with n=30, the normal approximation may be poor
